prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a product-pack shot for **Rolaids antacid tablets**. Two Rolaids packages are shown on a light tiled surface against a dark, vignetted background. The front package prominently displays the brand name “Rolaids,” the words “ANTACID TABLETS,” and an orange label reading “SODIUM FREE.” Large black text at the bottom of the frame reads **“100% RELIEF.”**

The image then cuts to a nearly blank, bright white frame. No product, person, or readable text is visible in this sampled frame; it appears to function as a visual transition.

The final sampled frame shows a different product display: **Benadryl** products arranged on a glossy blue surface with a light blue background. Visible items include two rectangular Benadryl boxes, a bottle in the center, and a smaller package or box positioned above/behind the bottle. The boxes feature the large brand name **“Benadryl”** with colored horizontal bands. At the bottom of the frame, black text reads **“From Parke-Davis.”** No people are visible in any of the sampled frames.
